title: Projects
paragraphs: These are list of what I've been working on, these are from my work or personal projects.

projects.title: Activity Scheduler
projects.description: My first side project, built with Laravel and Metronic Admin Panel.
paragraphs:
Around 2020, my coworker asked me if I could build a web application for his friend at clinic, he said his friend just need a simple activity scheduler.
Since I've never work on a side project before, I said yes and hoping I would be able to learn something new.

So, what this application does is like a task list for activities, if they've done it, then mark as done, otherwise mark as not done and provide a reason why.
It was a fun project because if I remember it correctly, at the time I was using Livewire and learned so much about it.

caption.title: Activity Detail
caption.description: To set the activity date and time

caption.title: Activities Checklist
caption.description: To mark the activity whether it's done or not

caption.title: Activity When It's Not Done
caption.description: Form to input the reasons why the activity is not done yet

caption.title: Activities Report
caption.description: Show activities and export the list

caption.title: Current User
caption.description: To change current user's name and password

projects.title: Facility Reservation
projects.description: It was my first project using Laravel (it is actually two applications at once, the other one is Asset Management).
paragraphs: I remembered when I first get a task to build a Facility Reservation for Purwakarta District Goverment (from work, we are application's vendor), It was my first time discovered Livewire and I was very excited to try it.

This also the time when Laravel 8 first released and introduced blade-component (I think) and I was super excited of these new technologies, so I build this with a struggle (because I never really tried Laravel on a real projects before just for learning purposes).

It was not my proudest project I've ever been working on because I never really use or understand blade-component and it makes my code messy and there was so much repetition for an element (see the images for context).
But this project make me want to use Laravel even more, to learn and really try to understand it correctly by implementing best practices, etc.
